perturbation.original_text:
13. INDEMNIFICATION
A. CONTENT PROVIDER shall, at its sole cost and expense, indemnify, save and hold harmless COMPANY and its successors, subdistributors, sublicensees, assigns, agents, representatives and affiliates from and against any and all claims, demands, causes of action, liability, loss, damage, cost and expense (including reasonable attorney's fees and court costs) incurred or sustained by reason of or arising out of any breach or alleged breach of any of the warranties, representations or agreements herein made by CONTENT PROVIDER, or by reason of any action, claim or proceeding related to or arising out of such breach or alleged breach by CONTENT PROVIDER. In the event that any person or entity shall make any claim or institute any suit or proceeding, COMPANY shall notify CONTENT PROVIDER in writing, and CONTENT PROVIDER must assume, at it own cost and expense, the defense thereof; provided, however, that COMPANY'S failure to provide such notice shall not affect this indemnity unless CONTENT PROVIDER has been materially prejudiced by such failure. COMPANY may, at its sole discretion, engage its own counsel in connection with any such suit, claim or proceeding, and the cost thereof (including reasonable fees and expenses) shall be borne by CONTENT PROVIDER provided that CONTENT PROVIDER shall in any event fulfill its obligation to undertake COMPANY'S defense. The final control and disposition of any claim, whether by settlement, compromise or otherwise, shall remain with COMPANY pursuant to the terms of this indemnification paragraph. In the event that CONTENT PROVIDER fails to promptly make any required payment to COMPANY, COMPANY shall have the right to withhold for its own account any royalties or other monies payable to CONTENT PROVIDER by COMPANY pursuant to this Agreement or any other agreement between CONTENT PROVIDER and COMPANY.
perturbation.changed_text:
13. INDEMNIFICATION
A. CONTENT PROVIDER shall, at its sole cost and expense, indemnify, save and hold harmless COMPANY and its successors, subdistributors, sublicensees, assigns, agents, representatives and affiliates from and against any and all claims, demands, causes of action, liability, loss, damage, cost and expense (including reasonable attorney's fees and court costs) incurred or sustained by reason of or arising out of any breach or alleged breach of any of the warranties, representations or agreements herein made by CONTENT PROVIDER, or by reason of any action, claim or proceeding related to or arising out of such breach or alleged breach by CONTENT PROVIDER. In the event that any person or entity shall make any claim or institute any suit or proceeding, COMPANY shall notify CONTENT PROVIDER in writing within 10 days of learning of the claim, and CONTENT PROVIDER must assume, at it own cost and expense, the defense thereof; provided, however, that COMPANY'S failure to provide such notice shall affect this indemnity even if CONTENT PROVIDER has not been materially prejudiced by such failure. COMPANY may, at its sole discretion, engage its own counsel in connection with any such suit, claim or proceeding, and the cost thereof (including reasonable fees and expenses) shall not be borne by CONTENT PROVIDER. The final control and disposition of any claim, whether by settlement, compromise or otherwise, shall not remain with COMPANY pursuant to the terms of this indemnification paragraph. In the event that CONTENT PROVIDER fails to promptly make any required payment to COMPANY, COMPANY shall not have the right to withhold for its own account any royalties or other monies payable to CONTENT PROVIDER by COMPANY pursuant to this Agreement or any other agreement between CONTENT PROVIDER and COMPANY.
perturbation.explanation:
This change introduces multiple in-text contradictions within Section 13.A, regarding indemnification. Specifically:

*   **Notification Period:** The original text states that COMPANY'S failure to provide notice to CONTENT PROVIDER only affects the indemnity if CONTENT PROVIDER has been materially prejudiced. The changed text states that failure to provide notice within 10 days *will* affect the indemnity, regardless of prejudice.
*   **Counsel Costs:** The original text requires CONTENT PROVIDER to bear the cost of COMPANY's counsel, while the changed text stipulates that CONTENT PROVIDER will *not* bear these costs.
*   **Control and Disposition of Claim:** The original text states that the final control and disposition of any claim remains with COMPANY, while the modified text states that it does not. The origianl text gives Company the rights to withhold payments if the CONTENT PROVIDER fails to make any required payment, to no longer have such rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
13. INDEMNIFICATION
A. CONTENT PROVIDER shall, at its sole cost and expense, indemnify, save and hold harmless COMPANY and its successors, subdistributors, sublicensees, assigns, agents, representatives and affiliates from and against any and all claims, demands, causes of action, liability, loss, damage, cost and expense (including reasonable attorney's fees and court costs) incurred or sustained by reason of or arising out of any breach or alleged breach of any of the warranties, representations or agreements herein made by CONTENT PROVIDER, or by reason of any action, claim or proceeding related to or arising out of such breach or alleged breach by CONTENT PROVIDER. In the event that any person or entity shall make any claim or institute any suit or proceeding, COMPANY shall notify CONTENT PROVIDER in writing within 10 days of learning of the claim, and CONTENT PROVIDER must assume, at it own cost and expense, the defense thereof; provided, however, that COMPANY'S failure to provide such notice shall affect this indemnity even if CONTENT PROVIDER has not been materially prejudiced by such failure. COMPANY may, at its sole discretion, engage its own counsel in connection with any such suit, claim or proceeding, and the cost thereof (including reasonable fees and expenses) shall not be borne by CONTENT PROVIDER. The final control and disposition of any claim, whether by settlement, compromise or otherwise, shall not remain with COMPANY pursuant to the terms of this indemnification paragraph. In the event that CONTENT PROVIDER fails to promptly make any required payment to COMPANY, COMPANY shall not have the right to withhold for its own account any royalties or other monies payable to CONTENT PROVIDER by COMPANY pursuant to this Agreement or any other agreement between CONTENT PROVIDER and COMPANY.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several in-text contradictions within Section 13.A. First, the original text states that COMPANY'S failure to provide notice only affects the indemnity if CONTENT PROVIDER has been materially prejudiced. The changed text states that failure to provide notice within 10 days *will* affect the indemnity, regardless of prejudice, which is a direct contradiction. Second, the original text requires CONTENT PROVIDER to bear the cost of COMPANY's counsel, while the changed text stipulates that CONTENT PROVIDER will *not* bear these costs. This is also a direct contradiction. Third, the original text states that the final control and disposition of any claim remains with COMPANY, while the modified text states that it does not. Finally, The origianl text gives Company the rights to withhold payments if the CONTENT PROVIDER fails to make any required payment, to no longer have such rights, directly contradicting the earlier obligation of indemnification in the same section.
